definitions.text: To have a consequence or remnant. To cause or allow (something) to remain as available; to refrain from taking (something) away; to stop short of consuming or otherwise depleting (something) entirely.
examples: I left my car at home and took a bus to work.
The ants did not leave so much as a crumb of bread.

definitions.text: To cause or allow (something) to remain as available; to refrain from taking (something) away; to stop short of consuming or otherwise depleting (something) entirely.
examples: I left my car at home and took a bus to work.
The ants did not leave so much as a crumb of bread.
The lightning left her dazzled for several minutes.
Infantile paralysis left him lame for the rest of his life.

definitions.text: The action of the batsman not attempting to play at the ball.
examples: He made 45 leaves in his innings of 64.

definitions.text: The arrangement of balls in play that remains after a shot is made (which determines whether the next shooter — who may be either the same player, or an opponent — has good options, or only poor ones).
The tiles remaining on a player's rack after his or her turn.
examples: I didn't score much, but LING was a good leave.

definitions.text: Permission to be absent; time away from one's work.
examples: I've just been given three weeks' leave by my boss — I don't think I still have some leave owing to me.

definitions.text: To give leave to; allow; permit; let; grant.
